title: Cross-Platform Mobile App Shell & Navigation
description: Build the foundational mobile application framework supporting iOS and Android with shared navigation, authentication flow, and responsive layout optimized for both phones and ruggedized tablets.
acceptance_criteria: Given a field technician with an iOS or Android device, when they launch the app, then they see a login screen integrated with Azure AD SSO
Given an authenticated technician, when they navigate the app, then all primary functions are accessible within two taps from the home screen
Given a technician using a phone or tablet, when they view any screen, then the layout adapts responsively to the device form factor
definition_of_done: App builds and runs on iOS 16+ and Android 13+
UI tested on both phone and tablet form factors
Accessibility audit passed (WCAG 2.1 AA)
Performance baseline established (cold start < 3 seconds)
Reviewed by product owner

title: Offline Data Storage & Sync Engine
description: Implement a local data persistence layer and background synchronization engine that allows technicians to work without connectivity and reliably syncs all changes when a connection is restored, including conflict resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician in an area with no connectivity, when they complete a work order, then all data is persisted locally on the device
Given a technician whose device regains connectivity, when the sync engine runs, then all locally stored changes are uploaded to the server
Given two users editing the same work order offline, when both sync, then the system applies conflict resolution rules and notifies affected users
definition_of_done: Offline storage supports all work order data types including photos
Sync engine tested with simulated network interruptions
Conflict resolution logic documented and unit tested
Load tested with 200 concurrent sync operations
Reviewed by product owner

title: Mobile Work Order Completion
description: Enable field technicians to view, update, and mark work orders as complete directly from the mobile app, replacing paper forms with a digital workflow that captures all required fields.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician with an assigned work order, when they open it on their mobile device, then they see all work order details including customer, asset, and task information
Given a technician on-site, when they complete all required fields and submit, then the work order status changes to 'Completed' and enters the approval workflow
Given a technician working offline, when they complete a work order, then the completion is stored locally and synced when connectivity returns
definition_of_done: All work order fields are editable on mobile
Form validation prevents incomplete submissions
Unit and integration tests written and passing
UX review completed with field technician feedback
Reviewed by product owner

title: On-Site Photo Capture & Digital Sign-Off
description: Allow technicians to capture photos, collect digital signatures from customers, and complete compliance checklists directly within the mobile app during on-site work.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician on-site, when they tap the photo capture button, then the device camera opens and the photo is attached to the work order
Given a completed job, when the technician requests a customer sign-off, then a digital signature pad is presented and the signature is stored with the work order
Given a work order with compliance requirements, when the technician opens the checklist, then all required items are displayed and must be completed before submission
definition_of_done: Photo capture works on both iOS and Android cameras
Digital signatures are stored as image files linked to work orders
Compliance checklists are configurable per work order type
All captured artifacts sync correctly in offline mode
Reviewed by product owner

title: Work Order Creation & Multi-Trigger Support
description: Implement work order creation supporting multiple trigger sources: manual entry from customer calls, automatic creation from maintenance contracts, and an extensible architecture for future IoT sensor triggers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher receiving a customer call, when they enter work order details, then a new work order is created with all required fields populated
Given an active maintenance contract with a scheduled trigger, when the trigger date arrives, then a work order is automatically created and assigned to the dispatch queue
Given the system architecture, when a new trigger source is defined, then it can be added without modifying existing work order creation logic
definition_of_done: Manual and contract-triggered creation paths tested end-to-end
API endpoint documented for future IoT integration
Unit tests cover all creation paths
Reviewed by product owner

title: Asset Linkage with IBM Maximo
description: Enable work orders to be linked to specific customer assets managed in IBM Maximo, providing technicians with asset history, specifications, and maintenance records directly within the work order context.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher creating a work order, when they search for a customer asset, then matching assets from Maximo are displayed with key details
Given a work order linked to a Maximo asset, when a technician views the work order, then they can see the asset's maintenance history and specifications
Given the Maximo API is temporarily unavailable, when a user attempts to link an asset, then the system displays a graceful error and allows the work order to be saved without the link
definition_of_done: Maximo API integration tested with real and mock data
Asset search returns results within 3 seconds
Error handling covers API timeout and unavailability scenarios
Integration documented in API docs
Reviewed by product owner

title: Two-Level Approval Workflow
description: Implement the two-level approval workflow where completed work orders require supervisor sign-off followed by finance review before proceeding to invoicing in SAP.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician has submitted a completed work order, when the supervisor opens their approval queue, then the work order appears with all completion details for review
Given a supervisor has approved a work order, when finance opens their review queue, then the work order appears with cost details and supervisor approval timestamp
Given a reviewer rejects a work order, when the rejection is submitted with comments, then the work order is returned to the previous stage and the relevant party is notified
definition_of_done: Both approval levels function with email/Teams notifications
Rejection flow returns work orders to the correct stage
Approval audit trail is captured for compliance
Unit and integration tests passing
Reviewed by product owner

title: Digital Work Order Forms & Paper Elimination
description: Replace all paper-based work order forms with digital equivalents that capture the same data fields, enforce required entries, and feed directly into the approval and invoicing workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given the current paper work order form, when compared to the digital form, then all data fields from the paper version are present in the digital version
Given a technician filling out a digital form, when they miss a required field, then the form highlights the missing field and prevents submission
Given a completed digital form, when submitted, then the data flows directly to the approval workflow without manual re-entry
definition_of_done: All legacy paper form fields mapped to digital equivalents
Form validation rules documented and tested
Data flows end-to-end from form to approval queue
Reviewed by product owner

title: Automated Scheduling & Dispatch Board
description: Replace the manual whiteboard-based dispatch process with a digital scheduling board that allows dispatchers to view technician availability, assign work orders, and manage the daily schedule through an intuitive drag-and-drop interface.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher viewing the schedule board, when they see unassigned work orders, then they can drag and drop them onto available technician time slots
Given the dispatch board, when a dispatcher views it for the first time, then they can perform basic scheduling tasks within 30 minutes of introduction
Given multiple work orders for the same region, when the system suggests assignments, then it optimizes for travel time and technician skill match
definition_of_done: Dispatch board loads within 2 seconds with full day's schedule
Usability testing completed with 3+ dispatchers
Drag-and-drop works on desktop browsers (Chrome, Edge)
Training guide created for dispatch team
Reviewed by product owner

title: Real-Time Technician Location Map
description: Integrate with FleetComplete to display real-time technician GPS locations on the dispatch board map, enabling location-aware job assignment and route optimization.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher viewing the dispatch board, when they toggle the map view, then all active technician locations are displayed with real-time GPS data from FleetComplete
Given a technician's vehicle is moving, when the dispatcher views the map, then the technician's position updates within 30 seconds
Given the FleetComplete API is unavailable, when a dispatcher views the map, then a notification indicates location data is stale and shows the last known positions
definition_of_done: FleetComplete API integration tested with live and mock data
Map renders correctly with 40+ simultaneous technician markers
Graceful degradation when GPS data is unavailable
API rate limits documented and respected
Reviewed by product owner

title: Technician Notification & Job Acceptance
description: When a dispatcher assigns a work order, automatically notify the technician via push notification and allow them to accept or flag the assignment directly from their mobile device.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher assigns a work order to a technician, when the assignment is saved, then the technician receives a push notification within 60 seconds
Given a technician receives an assignment notification, when they tap it, then the work order details are displayed with Accept and Flag options
Given a technician flags an assignment, when they provide a reason, then the dispatcher is notified and the work order returns to the unassigned queue
definition_of_done: Push notifications work on both iOS and Android
Notification delivery tested under offline-to-online transition
Flag/accept flow tested end-to-end
Reviewed by product owner

title: Real-Time Parts Availability Check
description: Integrate with SAP S/4HANA inventory module to allow technicians and dispatchers to check real-time parts availability before committing to a job site visit.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician viewing a work order, when they tap 'Check Parts', then the system queries SAP and displays current stock levels for all required parts
Given parts are available at a nearby warehouse, when the technician views results, then the warehouse location and estimated pickup time are shown
Given the SAP API is slow or unavailable, when a parts check is initiated, then the system times out gracefully after 10 seconds and suggests the technician call the warehouse
definition_of_done: SAP S/4HANA inventory API integration tested with real and mock data
Response time under 5 seconds for 95th percentile queries
Error handling covers SAP downtime scenarios
API documented
Reviewed by product owner

title: Proactive Parts Verification Before Dispatch
description: Automatically verify required parts availability when a work order is about to be dispatched, alerting the dispatcher if any parts are unavailable and suggesting alternatives or rescheduling.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher is about to assign a work order, when the system checks parts availability, then a green/yellow/red indicator shows parts readiness status
Given required parts are unavailable, when the dispatcher views the alert, then alternative parts or estimated restock dates are suggested
Given a work order has been dispatched with a parts warning, when the technician views the order, then the parts risk is clearly flagged
definition_of_done: Parts verification runs automatically before dispatch confirmation
Alert system tested with various inventory scenarios
Dispatcher can override warnings with documented justification
Reviewed by product owner

title: SAP Inventory Data Sync & API Modernization
description: Replace legacy custom stored procedures with modern RESTful API integration to SAP S/4HANA for inventory, financials, and invoicing data exchange. Ensure the integration handles SAP updates without breaking.
acceptance_criteria: Given the new API integration is deployed, when SAP inventory data changes, then the platform reflects the update within 5 minutes
Given SAP performs a system update, when the integration is tested post-update, then no breaking changes occur due to versioned API contracts
Given a data discrepancy between SAP and the platform, when an admin investigates, then a reconciliation log shows the source and timing of the discrepancy
definition_of_done: All legacy stored procedures identified and replaced
API versioning strategy documented and implemented
Integration tested against SAP sandbox environment
Rollback plan documented
Reviewed by product owner

title: Customer Self-Service Portal
description: Build a web-based customer portal where customers can log in, view all their active and historical work orders, and see real-time status updates without needing to call the 1-800 number.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer with valid credentials, when they log into the portal, then they see a dashboard of all their active and recent work orders
Given a work order is in progress, when the customer views its detail page, then the current status, assigned technician, and estimated completion time are displayed
Given a customer without an account, when they visit the portal, then they can request access through a self-registration flow pending admin approval
definition_of_done: Portal accessible via modern web browsers (Chrome, Edge, Safari, Firefox)
Responsive design works on desktop and mobile browsers
Customer data filtered by authenticated customer's account only
Security review completed
Reviewed by product owner

title: Automated Customer Status Notifications
description: Implement automated notifications sent to customers at key work order milestones: technician dispatched, technician en route, job started, and job complete.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician is dispatched to a job, when the assignment is confirmed, then the customer receives a notification with the technician's name and estimated arrival window
Given a technician marks a job as complete, when the status updates, then the customer receives a completion notification with a summary of work performed
Given a customer's notification preferences, when a milestone is reached, then the notification is sent via the customer's preferred channel (email or SMS)
definition_of_done: Notifications sent within 2 minutes of milestone trigger
Email and SMS channels supported
Customer notification preferences configurable in the portal
Unsubscribe mechanism available
Reviewed by product owner

title: Customer Work Order History & Search
description: Provide customers with the ability to search and filter their historical work orders by date range, asset, status, and technician, with exportable reports for their own records.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer on the portal, when they navigate to the history section, then they can filter work orders by date range, asset, and status
Given search results are displayed, when the customer clicks export, then a CSV or PDF report is downloaded
Given a customer has hundreds of work orders, when they search, then results load within 3 seconds
definition_of_done: Search and filter functionality tested with large datasets
Export formats validated (CSV, PDF)
Performance tested with 1000+ work orders per customer
Reviewed by product owner

title: Operational Dispatch Dashboard
description: Build a real-time operational dashboard for the dispatch team showing current job statuses, technician utilization rates, unassigned work orders, and service level indicators.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher opens the dashboard, when the page loads, then they see real-time counts of active, completed, and unassigned work orders for the current day
Given work orders are being completed in the field, when the dashboard auto-refreshes, then the status updates appear within 60 seconds
Given a dispatcher wants to drill down, when they click a metric tile, then they are taken to the relevant filtered list of work orders
definition_of_done: Dashboard loads within 3 seconds
Auto-refresh interval configurable (default 60 seconds)
Tested with realistic data volumes (200+ active work orders)
Reviewed by product owner

title: Executive KPI Dashboard
description: Deliver an executive-level dashboard showing key performance indicators including average time-to-resolution, first-time fix rate, customer satisfaction trends, and technician utilization over configurable time periods.
acceptance_criteria: Given an executive opens the KPI dashboard, when the page loads, then they see average time-to-resolution and first-time fix rate for the selected time period
Given an executive selects a different time range, when the filter is applied, then all KPIs recalculate and display within 5 seconds
Given KPI data is available, when an executive views trends, then week-over-week and month-over-month comparisons are displayed as charts
definition_of_done: KPIs calculated from live work order data
Time range filters include: this week, this month, this quarter, custom range
Charts render correctly on desktop and tablet
Data accuracy validated against manual calculations
Reviewed by product owner

title: IT System Health Monitoring Dashboard
description: Provide the IT team with a system health monitoring dashboard showing platform availability, API response times, integration health status, sync queue depth, and automated alerting for anomalies.
acceptance_criteria: Given an IT admin opens the health dashboard, when the page loads, then they see current availability status for all platform components and integrations
Given an integration endpoint is down, when the health check detects the failure, then an alert is sent to the IT team within 5 minutes
Given historical health data is available, when an IT admin views the timeline, then they can see uptime/downtime patterns over the past 30 days
definition_of_done: Health checks run for all critical endpoints (SAP, Maximo, FleetComplete, Azure AD)
Alerting configured via email and Teams
Dashboard tested with simulated failure scenarios
Runbook created for common alert responses
Reviewed by product owner

title: Azure AD SSO Integration
description: Integrate all platform components with Contoso's existing Azure AD (Entra ID) tenant for single sign-on authentication, ensuring users authenticate once and access all platform modules seamlessly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user with a valid Contoso Azure AD account, when they navigate to any platform module, then they are authenticated via SSO without entering separate credentials
Given a user's Azure AD session expires, when they next access the platform, then they are redirected to the Azure AD login and returned to their original page after authentication
Given a user is not in the Contoso Azure AD tenant, when they attempt to access the platform, then access is denied with a clear error message
definition_of_done: SSO works for web portal, mobile app, and admin interfaces
Token refresh and session management tested
Multi-factor authentication supported when enabled in Azure AD
Security review completed
Reviewed by product owner

title: Role-Based Access Control Configuration
description: Implement RBAC across the platform with predefined roles (Technician, Dispatcher, Supervisor, Finance, Account Manager, IT Admin) that restrict access to data and features based on job function.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user with the Technician role, when they access the platform, then they can only view their assigned work orders and cannot see customer contract data
Given a user with the Account Manager role, when they access customer data, then they can view sensitive contract information
Given an IT admin, when they manage role assignments, then they can assign and revoke roles through an admin interface synced with Azure AD groups
definition_of_done: All six predefined roles configured and tested
Role assignments sync with Azure AD security groups
Unauthorized access attempts logged for audit
Penetration testing completed for role boundary enforcement
Reviewed by product owner

title: Sensitive Data Access Restriction
description: Implement fine-grained data access controls ensuring sensitive customer contract data is only visible to users with Account Manager role or above, with all access attempts logged.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user without Account Manager or higher role, when they attempt to access customer contract data, then the data is hidden and access is denied
Given an Account Manager, when they view a customer profile, then contract details including pricing and terms are visible
Given any attempt to access restricted data, when the attempt occurs, then it is logged with user identity and outcome (allowed/denied)
definition_of_done: Data classification labels applied to all sensitive fields
Access control tested for all six roles
Audit logging verified for both allowed and denied access
Reviewed by product owner

title: Compliance Document Capture & Storage
description: Implement secure storage for all compliance documentation captured on-site (photos, digital sign-offs, completed checklists) with metadata tagging, encryption at rest, and linkage to the originating work order.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician submits a work order with compliance documents, when the documents are stored, then each document is encrypted at rest and tagged with work order ID, timestamp, and technician ID
Given all customer data must reside in the US, when compliance documents are stored, then the storage account is verified to be in a US Azure region
Given a compliance document is uploaded, when it is saved, then it is immutable and cannot be modified or deleted by non-admin users
definition_of_done: Storage uses Azure Blob Storage with US-region constraint
Encryption at rest enabled and verified
Immutability policies configured
Storage tested with large photo files (up to 20MB each)
Reviewed by product owner

title: Seven-Year Retention & Audit Trail
description: Configure document retention policies ensuring all compliance records are retained for a minimum of seven years with a complete audit trail tracking who created, accessed, or modified each document.
acceptance_criteria: Given a compliance document stored in the system, when the retention period is checked, then it is set to a minimum of seven years from the creation date
Given any user accesses a compliance document, when the access occurs, then an audit log entry is created with user ID, timestamp, and action type
Given an IT admin queries the audit trail, when they search by work order or date range, then all access and modification events are returned
definition_of_done: Retention policies automated via Azure Storage lifecycle rules
Audit logs stored separately and tamper-proof
Retention compliance verified with sample data across 7-year simulated timeline
Reviewed by product owner

title: Teams Work Order Notifications
description: Integrate with Microsoft Teams to send work order notifications (new assignment, status changes, approval requests) directly to technicians and supervisors via Teams messages and adaptive cards.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician is assigned a new work order, when the assignment is saved, then they receive a Teams notification with an adaptive card showing work order summary
Given a supervisor has a pending approval, when the work order enters the approval queue, then they receive a Teams notification with approve/reject action buttons
Given a technician is using Teams on iOS or Android, when they receive a notification, then the adaptive card renders correctly on the mobile Teams client
definition_of_done: Teams bot/connector registered and configured
Adaptive cards tested on desktop and mobile Teams clients
Notification delivery latency under 60 seconds
Reviewed by product owner

title: Teams Work Order Interaction
description: Enable technicians to view work order details, update status, and perform basic actions (accept, flag, mark en route) directly from within Microsoft Teams without switching to the dedicated mobile app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician receives a work order notification in Teams, when they click 'View Details', then full work order information is displayed in a Teams task module
Given a technician viewing a work order in Teams, when they tap 'Accept' or 'Mark En Route', then the work order status updates in the platform in real time
Given a technician with no connectivity, when they attempt an action in Teams, then the action is queued and a message indicates it will be processed when connectivity returns
definition_of_done: Teams task modules render work order details correctly
Status update actions sync with the platform backend
Tested on iOS and Android Teams clients
No additional app installation required beyond Teams
Reviewed by product owner

title: Azure Infrastructure Provisioning & US Data Residency
description: Provision the Azure-native hosting infrastructure in US regions, configure networking, storage, and compute resources, and enforce data residency policies ensuring all customer data remains within the United States.
acceptance_criteria: Given the infrastructure is provisioned, when Azure resource locations are audited, then all resources hosting customer data are in US Azure regions
Given a deployment attempt to a non-US region, when the deployment runs, then Azure Policy denies the deployment and logs the violation
Given the infrastructure requirements, when the environment is provisioned, then it is defined as Infrastructure as Code (Bicep/Terraform) for reproducibility
definition_of_done: Infrastructure as Code templates created and version controlled
Azure Policy for US-only data residency enforced
Network security groups and firewall rules configured
Cost estimate documented and approved
Reviewed by product owner

title: Phased Rollout & Parallel Operation Support
description: Implement the phased rollout strategy enabling the new platform to run in parallel with the legacy system during the transition period, including data synchronization between old and new systems and a clear cutover plan.
acceptance_criteria: Given the pilot region is identified, when the new platform is deployed, then both legacy and new systems operate simultaneously for the pilot group
Given work orders are created in either system during parallel operation, when data is synced, then both systems reflect consistent work order status
Given the pilot validation is complete, when the cutover decision is made, then a documented runbook guides the transition from legacy to new platform
definition_of_done: Parallel operation tested with pilot region data
Data sync between legacy and new systems validated
Cutover runbook documented and reviewed
Rollback plan documented
Reviewed by product owner

title: SAP S/4HANA API Integration Layer
description: Build the modern API integration layer replacing legacy stored procedures for SAP S/4HANA connectivity, supporting financials, inventory, and invoicing data exchange with versioned APIs resilient to SAP updates.
acceptance_criteria: Given the integration layer is deployed, when the platform sends financial data to SAP, then invoices are created correctly in SAP S/4HANA
Given SAP performs a quarterly update, when the integration is tested, then all API contracts remain functional due to versioning
Given an API call fails, when the integration layer retries, then it follows an exponential backoff strategy and logs the failure for IT monitoring
definition_of_done: All legacy stored procedures replaced with RESTful API calls
API versioning implemented (v1 baseline)
Retry and circuit breaker patterns implemented
Integration tested against SAP sandbox
Comprehensive API documentation published
Reviewed by product owner

title: API Documentation & Developer Portal
description: Create comprehensive API documentation for all custom-built platform components, published through a developer portal that enables internal teams to understand, test, and integrate with the platform APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a developer accessing the API portal, when they browse available endpoints, then they see complete request/response schemas with examples for every custom API
Given API documentation, when a developer reads an endpoint description, then they can successfully make a test call using the provided examples
Given a new API endpoint is deployed, when the deployment completes, then the documentation is automatically updated via the CI/CD pipeline
definition_of_done: OpenAPI/Swagger specifications generated for all custom APIs
Developer portal hosted and accessible to internal teams
Auto-generation integrated into CI/CD pipeline
Documentation reviewed by development team for accuracy
Reviewed by product owner
